accessibility_gaps: No token specified for the 'selected' state indicator (tokens_referenced is empty); if implemented as a color-only change it fails WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Color and needs a non-color cue plus aria-pressed/aria-selected/aria-checked

color.border.default (#D8DEE9) on color.surface.card/color.background.default (#FFFFFF) = ~1.3:1, FAILS the 3:1 minimum for UI component/non-text borders (WCAG 1.4.11) if used alone to convey the card's default or selected boundary
color.text.disabled (#94A3B8) on white = ~2.8:1, FAILS 4.5:1 for body text — must not be used for masked card number, expiry, or cardholder name in the default (non-disabled) state

If color.action.primary.default (#1A5CFF) is adopted as an ad-hoc 'selected' border on white it is ~4.0:1 (passes 3:1 for non-text/UI), but this is an unmapped/guessed token choice since no selection token exists in the design system reference for this component

No semantic role specified for the card's selectable behavior (e.g., role="radio"/aria-checked within role="radiogroup", or aria-selected within role="listbox"/role="group", or aria-pressed if a toggle button) — brief only says 'user can select the card'
Masked card number ('**** **** **** 1234') has no accessible-name strategy; screen readers may read masking glyphs literally instead of an announcement like 'Card ending in 1234'

Card brand icon (Visa/Mastercard/Amex) has no accessible-name requirement; per the Icon component's aria-hidden default it risks being unlabeled instead of exposing the brand as an accessible name or visually-hidden text

Delete action (icon-only) has no aria-label requirement (e.g., 'Delete card ending in 1234') and no confirmation step (role="alertdialog") despite being a destructive, hard-to-reverse action on sensitive payment data
No aria-live/role="status" specified to announce the result of select or delete actions to screen reader users
No visible focus ring specified for the selectable card region or the delete control (should use color.focus.ring)
No keyboard interaction model specified: Enter/Space to select the card, Tab order between card body and delete control, Escape to dismiss any delete-confirmation surface
No requirement that the delete icon-button remain independently focusable/operable and not nested as interactive-in-interactive with the card's own select action
No prefers-reduced-motion guidance for the selection-state transition (e.g., highlight/border animation) or for any card-removal animation on delete
No async/loading state defined for the delete action (e.g., Spinner while a delete request is in flight) or its announcement via role="status"
recommendations: Add an explicit 'selected' visual token/spec (e.g., border-color: var(--color-action-primary-default) at >=2px plus a checkmark icon) so selection is never conveyed by color alone

Model the card group as role="radiogroup" of role="radio" cards (or role="listbox"/aria-selected items) with aria-checked/aria-selected bound to the `selected` state prop, and support Space/Enter to select
Give the masked number an aria-label (e.g., 'Card ending in 1234') separate from its visible masked text, and expose the brand icon's accessible name (e.g., aria-label="Visa") rather than aria-hidden

Make the delete control a distinct, independently focusable icon-button with aria-label="Delete card ending in {last4}", require a confirmation dialog (role="alertdialog") before removal, and announce success/failure via role="status"
Apply var(--color-focus-ring) as a visible focus-visible outline on both the selectable card and the delete button
Wrap any selection or removal animation in `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` to disable/shorten it, and show a Spinner with role="status" while a delete request is pending
